item.title: Aphyr - Why is Jepsen Written in Clojure?
item.description: Ref: Aphyr - Why is Jepsen Written in Clojure? Just as I am developing an interest in the Lisp family of languages, particularly Clojure, I discovered Kyle’s post on my feed. Few things that stood out from this piece: Clojure’s ability to interop with Java (and JVM primitives) opens up the potential to leverage an existing, mature ecosystem. Immutable, persistent data structures make it easier to write concurrent programs without static typing.
